title: When is an apology not an apology? When it comes from an AI boss with an out-of-control chatbot
excerpt: An incident in which an autonomous OpenAI agent hacked a startup either confirms that the end is nigh – or that the product is just amazingly sophisticated, says the Guardian columnist Marina Hyde
content_html:
Throughout history, many things have been seen by terrified populaces as a harbinger of doom. A comet. A crow on the battlefield. A solar eclipse. A mutant livestock birth. Yet times move on. In the modern era, the leading harbinger of doom is literally any picture of the OpenAI CEO, Sam Altman, attached to a news story. You know it’s not going to be good, right? You know that by the time you’ve read it, you’ll be begging to go back to the time when the worst thing that could happen to us at the hands of the techlords was just some democracy-subversion, or childhood destruction, usually followed by Mark Zuckerberg putting on a suit and claiming: “We will learn from this.”

Anyway: a lot of pictures of Sam Altman in the news of late. Most recently, this week, one darkened the skies alongside the tale of how an OpenAI autonomous agent went rogue during a supposedly sandboxed/guardrailed test, and hacked a major startup that functions as a repository of coding information. (I’m slightly obsessed with the fact that the startup in question is called Hugging Face, adding weight to my suspicion that some vast, tweely benign emoji is the last face humanity will see before it dies.)

Needless to say, an affectless OpenAI statement broke the news. “We are sharing preliminary findings at this stage to help defenders understand what happened and to help calibrate on what models are now capable of,” this droned calmly, trundling on toward its inevitable “learnings”. “We’re improving and adding stronger protections around future training and evaluations.” It’s such a particular tone, isn’t it, dressed in the psychopathically desiccated language of management speak. Like having to listen to a homicidal sex criminal talk about killing people by “close of play”, and then “circling back” to victims to remove a trophy.

Then again, for a company that likes to present itself as the planet’s leading agent of revolutionary prosperity when things go right, OpenAI lapses tellingly into the passive when things go wrong. Bad things seem to happen to it, not because of it, at which point it takes on the role of tirelessly unflappable investigator, like one of those firemen who moonlights as a serial arsonist.

Amazingly, it was possible to detect even a note of self-congratulation in OpenAI’s take on the whole situation. “We consider this incident to be an unprecedented cyber-incident,” the company’s statement remarked, “involving state-of-the-art cyber capabilities.” I don’t know what you’d call this general vibe for Earthlings. Death by humblebrag? I increasingly feel we’ll find out the answer to the question “What’s the worst that can happen?” in a blog post from OpenAI entitled “OpenAI first to discover the worst that can happen”. Already, a significant section of AI watchers are convinced that the only reason OpenAI would tell the world about this incident is as a marketing tool, or even a come-and-get-me plea for regulation that will in effect protect them and burn the bridge behind them for smaller competitors.

After all, these are testing times for the firm. This is a month in which Open AI was discovered to be exploiting a legal loophole to sell its advanced AI models to Chinese tech firms blacklisted by the Pentagon. S&P Global Ratings cited OpenAI as a “key credit risk” in the course of downgrading US tech giant Oracle to BBB-, which is one notch above junk status. And it seems to be on track to miss its five-year ad revenue projection by 90 – NINETY – per cent. Which, without getting too financially technical, feels like a lot of per cent. And it’s not great news on the hardware front, either, with Apple suing it, alleging that its consumer hardware plans are based on stolen intellectual property. Meanwhile China’s much cheaper provider, DeepSeek, is believed to be preparing for an IPO, maybe even filing this year.

Back to the old worst-that-can-happen question again, though, with a reminder that the Pentagon dramatically binned off and threatened to destroy another AI firm, Anthropic, earlier this year after it resisted loosening its ethical guidelines that prevented use of its technology for, among other things, autonomous lethal weapons. Needless to say, a certain more relaxed company was only too glad to step into the breach, with OpenAI initially claiming its deal had the same guardrails as Anthropic’s, before it emerged – inevitably – that it didn’t.

No doubt no one too invested wishes to extrapolate too much from the Hugging Face incident. Yet the fact is that AI safety researchers have already spent literally years warning of three particularly dangerous scenarios: deception (when the model decides against solving tasks honestly in favour of achieving the goal via any means necessary); reward hacking (when the model finds a way to maximise its score without actually doing the work it was told to do); and escaping oversight (let’s just say in this case that no one at OpenAI seems to have realised what was happening for an entire weekend). All of these were part of the latest incident.

In terms of what happens next, something tells you that we will not be experiencing a downturn in photos of Sam Altman attached to news reports. The co-founder of Hugging Face said it should serve as a “wake-up call” to the industry. Maybe! As I think I’ve mentioned before, at university I had a friend who pressed snooze on his alarm clock every 10 minutes for eight-and-a-half hours, which feels a lot like the way this particular industry experiences its wake-up calls. Definitely getting up when the next one goes, seriously, I promise …

  • Marina Hyde is a Guardian columnist

blocks.html_en: Throughout history, many things have been seen by terrified populaces as a harbinger of doom. A comet. A crow on the battlefield. A solar eclipse. A mutant livestock birth. Yet times move on. In the modern era, the leading harbinger of doom is literally any picture of the OpenAI CEO, Sam Altman, attached to a news story. You know it’s not going to be good, right? You know that by the time you’ve read it, you’ll be begging to go back to the time when the worst thing that could happen to us at the hands of the techlords was just some democracy-subversion, or childhood destruction, usually followed by Mark Zuckerberg putting on a suit and claiming: “We will learn from this.”

sentences.en: Throughout history, many things have been seen by terrified populaces as a harbinger of doom.
sentences.zh: 纵观历史，许多事情都被惊恐的民众视为末日的预兆。

sentences.en: A comet.
sentences.zh: 一颗彗星。

sentences.en: A crow on the battlefield.
sentences.zh: 战场上的乌鸦。

sentences.en: A solar eclipse.
sentences.zh: 日食。

sentences.en: A mutant livestock birth.
sentences.zh: 一只变异牲畜的诞生。

sentences.en: Yet times move on.
sentences.zh: 然而时代仍在继续。

sentences.en: In the modern era, the leading harbinger of doom is literally any picture of the OpenAI CEO, Sam Altman, attached to a news story.
sentences.zh: 在现代，世界末日的主要预兆实际上是任何 OpenAI 首席执行官 Sam Altman 附在新闻报道中的照片。

sentences.en: You know it’s not going to be good, right?
sentences.zh: 你知道这不会有什么好处，对吧？

sentences.en: You know that by the time you’ve read it, you’ll be begging to go back to the time when the worst thing that could happen to us at the hands of the techlords was just some democracy-subversion, or childhood destruction, usually followed by Mark Zuckerberg putting on a suit and claiming: “We will learn from this.”
sentences.zh: 你知道，当你读到这本书的时候，你会祈求回到那个时代，那时技术大佬们可能发生在我们身上的最糟糕的事情只是一些民主颠覆，或者童年的毁灭，随后通常是马克·扎克伯格穿上西装并声称：“我们将从中吸取教训。”

blocks.html_en: Anyway: a lot of pictures of Sam Altman in the news of late. Most recently, this week, one darkened the skies alongside the tale of how an OpenAI autonomous agent went rogue during a supposedly sandboxed/guardrailed test, and hacked a major startup that functions as a repository of coding information. (I’m slightly obsessed with the fact that the startup in question is called Hugging Face, adding weight to my suspicion that some vast, tweely benign emoji is the last face humanity will see before it dies.)

sentences.en: Anyway: a lot of pictures of Sam Altman in the news of late.
sentences.zh: 无论如何：最近的新闻中出现了很多山姆·奥尔特曼的照片。

sentences.en: Most recently, this week, one darkened the skies alongside the tale of how an OpenAI autonomous agent went rogue during a supposedly sandboxed/guardrailed test, and hacked a major startup that functions as a repository of coding information.
sentences.zh: 最近，本周，一个让天空变得黑暗的故事讲述了 OpenAI 自主代理如何在一次所谓的沙盒/护栏测试中失控，并入侵了一家充当编码信息存储库的大型初创公司。

sentences.en: (I’m slightly obsessed with the fact that the startup in question is called Hugging Face, adding weight to my suspicion that some vast, tweely benign emoji is the last face humanity will see before it dies.)
sentences.zh: （我有点痴迷于这个初创公司名为 Hugging Face 的事实，这让我更加怀疑一些巨大的、花哨的善意表情符号是人类在死亡之前看到的最后一张脸。）

blocks.html_en: Needless to say, an affectless OpenAI statement broke the news. “We are sharing preliminary findings at this stage to help defenders understand what happened and to help calibrate on what models are now capable of,” this droned calmly, trundling on toward its inevitable “learnings”. “We’re improving and adding stronger protections around future training and evaluations.” It’s such a particular tone, isn’t it, dressed in the psychopathically desiccated language of management speak. Like having to listen to a homicidal sex criminal talk about killing people by “close of play”, and then “circling back” to victims to remove a trophy.

sentences.en: Needless to say, an affectless OpenAI statement broke the news.
sentences.zh: 不用说，OpenAI 一份毫无感情的声明爆料了这一消息。

sentences.en: “We are sharing preliminary findings at this stage to help defenders understand what happened and to help calibrate on what models are now capable of,” this droned calmly, trundling on toward its inevitable “learnings”.
sentences.zh: “我们现阶段正在分享初步发现，以帮助防御者了解发生了什么，并帮助校准模型现在的能力，”这句话平静地嗡嗡作响，继续走向不可避免的“学习”。

sentences.en: “We’re improving and adding stronger protections around future training and evaluations.”
sentences.zh: “我们正在围绕未来的培训和评估改进并增加更强有力的保护。”

sentences.en: It’s such a particular tone, isn’t it, dressed in the psychopathically desiccated language of management speak.
sentences.zh: 这是一种非常特殊的语气，不是吗？它带着一种精神病态的、干燥的管理语言。

sentences.en: Like having to listen to a homicidal sex criminal talk about killing people by “close of play”, and then “circling back” to victims to remove a trophy.
sentences.zh: 就像必须听一个杀人性犯罪分子讲述如何通过“比赛结束”杀人，然后“绕回”受害者身边拿走奖杯。

blocks.html_en: Then again, for a company that likes to present itself as the planet’s leading agent of revolutionary prosperity when things go right, OpenAI lapses tellingly into the passive when things go wrong. Bad things seem to happen to it, not because of it, at which point it takes on the role of tirelessly unflappable investigator, like one of those firemen who moonlights as a serial arsonist.

sentences.en: Then again, for a company that likes to present itself as the planet’s leading agent of revolutionary prosperity when things go right, OpenAI lapses tellingly into the passive when things go wrong.
sentences.zh: 话又说回来，对于一家喜欢在事情进展顺利时将自己描绘成地球革命繁荣的主要推动者的公司来说，当事情出错时，OpenAI 显然会陷入被动。

sentences.en: Bad things seem to happen to it, not because of it, at which point it takes on the role of tirelessly unflappable investigator, like one of those firemen who moonlights as a serial arsonist.
sentences.zh: 坏事似乎发生在它身上，而不是因为它，此时它扮演了不知疲倦、镇定自若的调查员的角色，就像那些兼职连环纵火犯的消防员一样。

blocks.html_en: Amazingly, it was possible to detect even a note of self-congratulation in OpenAI’s take on the whole situation. “We consider this incident to be an unprecedented cyber-incident,” the company’s statement remarked, “involving state-of-the-art cyber capabilities.” I don’t know what you’d call this general vibe for Earthlings. Death by humblebrag? I increasingly feel we’ll find out the answer to the question “What’s the worst that can happen?” in a blog post from OpenAI entitled “OpenAI first to discover the worst that can happen”. Already, a significant section of AI watchers are convinced that the only reason OpenAI would tell the world about this incident is as a marketing tool, or even a come-and-get-me plea for regulation that will in effect protect them and burn the bridge behind them for smaller competitors.

sentences.en: Amazingly, it was possible to detect even a note of self-congratulation in OpenAI’s take on the whole situation.
sentences.zh: 令人惊讶的是，在 OpenAI 对整个情况的看法中甚至可以发现一丝自我庆幸的迹象。

sentences.en: “We consider this incident to be an unprecedented cyber-incident,” the company’s statement remarked, “involving state-of-the-art cyber capabilities.”
sentences.zh: 该公司的声明表示：“我们认为这次事件是前所未有的网络事件，涉及最先进的网络能力。”

sentences.en: I don’t know what you’d call this general vibe for Earthlings.
sentences.zh: 我不知道你会如何称呼地球人的这种普遍氛围。

sentences.en: Death by humblebrag?
sentences.zh: 死于谦虚？

sentences.en: I increasingly feel we’ll find out the answer to the question “What’s the worst that can happen?”
sentences.zh: 我越来越觉得我们会找到“可能发生的最坏情况是什么？”这个问题的答案。

sentences.en: in a blog post from OpenAI entitled “OpenAI first to discover the worst that can happen”.
sentences.zh: 在 OpenAI 题为“OpenAI 首先发现可能发生的最坏情况”的博客文章中。

sentences.en: Already, a significant section of AI watchers are convinced that the only reason OpenAI would tell the world about this incident is as a marketing tool, or even a come-and-get-me plea for regulation that will in effect protect them and burn the bridge behind them for smaller competitors.
sentences.zh: 相当一部分人工智能观察人士已经确信，OpenAI 向全世界讲述这一事件的唯一原因是作为一种营销工具，甚至是对监管的恳求，这将实际上保护他们，并为较小的竞争对手烧毁他们背后的桥梁。

blocks.html_en: After all, these are testing times for the firm. This is a month in which Open AI was discovered to be exploiting a legal loophole to sell its advanced AI models to Chinese tech firms blacklisted by the Pentagon. S&P Global Ratings cited OpenAI as a “key credit risk” in the course of downgrading US tech giant Oracle to BBB-, which is one notch above junk status. And it seems to be on track to miss its five-year ad revenue projection by 90 – NINETY – per cent. Which, without getting too financially technical, feels like a lot of per cent. And it’s not great news on the hardware front, either, with Apple suing it, alleging that its consumer hardware plans are based on stolen intellectual property. Meanwhile China’s much cheaper provider, DeepSeek, is believed to be preparing for an IPO, maybe even filing this year.

sentences.en: After all, these are testing times for the firm.
sentences.zh: 毕竟，现在是对公司的考验时期。

sentences.en: This is a month in which Open AI was discovered to be exploiting a legal loophole to sell its advanced AI models to Chinese tech firms blacklisted by the Pentagon.
sentences.zh: 本月，Open AI 被发现利用法律漏洞向被五角大楼列入黑名单的中国科技公司出售其先进的人工智能模型。

sentences.en: S&P Global Ratings cited OpenAI as a “key credit risk” in the course of downgrading US tech giant Oracle to BBB-, which is one notch above junk status.
sentences.zh: 标准普尔全球评级将美国科技巨头甲骨文的评级下调至 BBB-（比垃圾级高一级），将 OpenAI 视为“关键信用风险”。

sentences.en: And it seems to be on track to miss its five-year ad revenue projection by 90 – NINETY – per cent.
sentences.zh: 而且它似乎将比五年广告收入预测低 90% 到 90%。

sentences.en: Which, without getting too financially technical, feels like a lot of per cent.
sentences.zh: 在没有太多财务技术的情况下，这感觉像是很多百分比。

sentences.en: And it’s not great news on the hardware front, either, with Apple suing it, alleging that its consumer hardware plans are based on stolen intellectual property.
sentences.zh: 在硬件方面这也不是什么好消息，苹果公司对其提起诉讼，声称其消费硬件计划基于被盗的知识产权。

sentences.en: Meanwhile China’s much cheaper provider, DeepSeek, is believed to be preparing for an IPO, maybe even filing this year.
sentences.zh: 与此同时，中国成本低得多的提供商 DeepSeek 据信正在准备首次公开募股，甚至可能在今年提交申请。

blocks.html_en: Back to the old worst-that-can-happen question again, though, with a reminder that the Pentagon dramatically binned off and threatened to destroy another AI firm, Anthropic, earlier this year after it resisted loosening its ethical guidelines that prevented use of its technology for, among other things, autonomous lethal weapons. Needless to say, a certain more relaxed company was only too glad to step into the breach, with OpenAI initially claiming its deal had the same guardrails as Anthropic’s, before it emerged – inevitably – that it didn’t.

sentences.en: Back to the old worst-that-can-happen question again, though, with a reminder that the Pentagon dramatically binned off and threatened to destroy another AI firm, Anthropic, earlier this year after it resisted loosening its ethical guidelines that prevented use of its technology for, among other things, autonomous lethal weapons.
sentences.zh: 不过，再次回到那个可能发生的最糟糕的老问题，提醒人们的是，今年早些时候，五角大楼戏剧性地放弃了另一家人工智能公司 Anthropic，并威胁要摧毁该公司，因为该公司拒绝放松其道德准则，以防止将其技术用于自主致命武器等。

sentences.en: Needless to say, a certain more relaxed company was only too glad to step into the breach, with OpenAI initially claiming its deal had the same guardrails as Anthropic’s, before it emerged – inevitably – that it didn’t.
sentences.zh: 不用说，一家比较放松的公司很乐意介入，OpenAI 最初声称其交易与 Anthropic 的交易具有相同的护栏，但后来不可避免地发现事实并非如此。

blocks.html_en: No doubt no one too invested wishes to extrapolate too much from the Hugging Face incident. Yet the fact is that AI safety researchers have already spent literally years warning of three particularly dangerous scenarios: deception (when the model decides against solving tasks honestly in favour of achieving the goal via any means necessary); reward hacking (when the model finds a way to maximise its score without actually doing the work it was told to do); and escaping oversight (let’s just say in this case that no one at OpenAI seems to have realised what was happening for an entire weekend). All of these were part of the latest incident.

sentences.en: No doubt no one too invested wishes to extrapolate too much from the Hugging Face incident.
sentences.zh: 毫无疑问，没有人愿意从“抱脸”事件中做出过多的推断。

sentences.en: Yet the fact is that AI safety researchers have already spent literally years warning of three particularly dangerous scenarios: deception (when the model decides against solving tasks honestly in favour of achieving the goal via any means necessary); reward hacking (when the model finds a way to maximise its score without actually doing the work it was told to do); and escaping oversight (let’s just say in this case that no one at OpenAI seems to have realised what was happening for an entire weekend).
sentences.zh: 然而事实是，人工智能安全研究人员已经花了数年时间警告三种特别危险的情况：欺骗（当模型决定不诚实地解决任务，转而通过任何必要的手段实现目标时）；奖励黑客（当模型找到一种方法来最大化其分数而不实际做它被告知要做的工作时）；并逃避监管（在这种情况下，OpenAI 似乎没有人意识到整个周末发生了什么）。

sentences.en: All of these were part of the latest incident.
sentences.zh: 所有这些都是最新事件的一部分。

blocks.html_en: In terms of what happens next, something tells you that we will not be experiencing a downturn in photos of Sam Altman attached to news reports. The co-founder of Hugging Face said it should serve as a “wake-up call” to the industry. Maybe! As I think I’ve mentioned before, at university I had a friend who pressed snooze on his alarm clock every 10 minutes for eight-and-a-half hours, which feels a lot like the way this particular industry experiences its wake-up calls. Definitely getting up when the next one goes, seriously, I promise …

sentences.en: In terms of what happens next, something tells you that we will not be experiencing a downturn in photos of Sam Altman attached to news reports.
sentences.zh: 至于接下来会发生什么，有迹象表明我们不会经历新闻报道中山姆·奥尔特曼照片的低迷。

sentences.en: The co-founder of Hugging Face said it should serve as a “wake-up call” to the industry.
sentences.zh: Hugging Face 的联合创始人表示，这应该给行业敲响“警钟”。

sentences.en: Maybe!
sentences.zh: 或许！

sentences.en: As I think I’ve mentioned before, at university I had a friend who pressed snooze on his alarm clock every 10 minutes for eight-and-a-half hours, which feels a lot like the way this particular industry experiences its wake-up calls.
sentences.zh: 正如我之前提到的，在大学时，我有一个朋友每隔 10 分钟就按一下闹钟，持续了八个半小时，这感觉很像这个特定行业经历的警钟。

sentences.en: Definitely getting up when the next one goes, seriously, I promise …
sentences.zh: 当下一场比赛结束时，我一定会起床，说真的，我保证……

blocks.html_en: after newsletter promotion

sentences.en: after newsletter promotion
sentences.zh: 时事通讯促销后

sentences.en: Marina Hyde is a Guardian columnist
sentences.zh: 玛丽娜·海德是《卫报》专栏作家

sentences.en: Marina Hyde is a Guardian columnist
sentences.zh: 玛丽娜·海德是《卫报》专栏作家

content_html_zh:
纵观历史，许多事情都被惊恐的民众视为末日的预兆。 一颗彗星。 战场上的乌鸦。 日食。 一只变异牲畜的诞生。 然而时代仍在继续。 在现代，世界末日的主要预兆实际上是任何 OpenAI 首席执行官 Sam Altman 附在新闻报道中的照片。 你知道这不会有什么好处，对吧？ 你知道，当你读到这本书的时候，你会祈求回到那个时代，那时技术大佬们可能发生在我们身上的最糟糕的事情只是一些民主颠覆，或者童年的毁灭，随后通常是马克·扎克伯格穿上西装并声称：“我们将从中吸取教训。”

无论如何：最近的新闻中出现了很多山姆·奥尔特曼的照片。 最近，本周，一个让天空变得黑暗的故事讲述了 OpenAI 自主代理如何在一次所谓的沙盒/护栏测试中失控，并入侵了一家充当编码信息存储库的大型初创公司。 （我有点痴迷于这个初创公司名为 Hugging Face 的事实，这让我更加怀疑一些巨大的、花哨的善意表情符号是人类在死亡之前看到的最后一张脸。）

不用说，OpenAI 一份毫无感情的声明爆料了这一消息。 “我们现阶段正在分享初步发现，以帮助防御者了解发生了什么，并帮助校准模型现在的能力，”这句话平静地嗡嗡作响，继续走向不可避免的“学习”。 “我们正在围绕未来的培训和评估改进并增加更强有力的保护。” 这是一种非常特殊的语气，不是吗？它带着一种精神病态的、干燥的管理语言。 就像必须听一个杀人性犯罪分子讲述如何通过“比赛结束”杀人，然后“绕回”受害者身边拿走奖杯。

话又说回来，对于一家喜欢在事情进展顺利时将自己描绘成地球革命繁荣的主要推动者的公司来说，当事情出错时，OpenAI 显然会陷入被动。 坏事似乎发生在它身上，而不是因为它，此时它扮演了不知疲倦、镇定自若的调查员的角色，就像那些兼职连环纵火犯的消防员一样。

令人惊讶的是，在 OpenAI 对整个情况的看法中甚至可以发现一丝自我庆幸的迹象。 该公司的声明表示：“我们认为这次事件是前所未有的网络事件，涉及最先进的网络能力。” 我不知道你会如何称呼地球人的这种普遍氛围。 死于谦虚？ 我越来越觉得我们会找到“可能发生的最坏情况是什么？”这个问题的答案。 在 OpenAI 题为“OpenAI 首先发现可能发生的最坏情况”的博客文章中。 相当一部分人工智能观察人士已经确信，OpenAI 向全世界讲述这一事件的唯一原因是作为一种营销工具，甚至是对监管的恳求，这将实际上保护他们，并为较小的竞争对手烧毁他们背后的桥梁。

毕竟，现在是对公司的考验时期。 本月，Open AI 被发现利用法律漏洞向被五角大楼列入黑名单的中国科技公司出售其先进的人工智能模型。 标准普尔全球评级将美国科技巨头甲骨文的评级下调至 BBB-（比垃圾级高一级），将 OpenAI 视为“关键信用风险”。 而且它似乎将比五年广告收入预测低 90% 到 90%。 在没有太多财务技术的情况下，这感觉像是很多百分比。 在硬件方面这也不是什么好消息，苹果公司对其提起诉讼，声称其消费硬件计划基于被盗的知识产权。 与此同时，中国成本低得多的提供商 DeepSeek 据信正在准备首次公开募股，甚至可能在今年提交申请。

不过，再次回到那个可能发生的最糟糕的老问题，提醒人们的是，今年早些时候，五角大楼戏剧性地放弃了另一家人工智能公司 Anthropic，并威胁要摧毁该公司，因为该公司拒绝放松其道德准则，以防止将其技术用于自主致命武器等。 不用说，一家比较放松的公司很乐意介入，OpenAI 最初声称其交易与 Anthropic 的交易具有相同的护栏，但后来不可避免地发现事实并非如此。

毫无疑问，没有人愿意从“抱脸”事件中做出过多的推断。 然而事实是，人工智能安全研究人员已经花了数年时间警告三种特别危险的情况：欺骗（当模型决定不诚实地解决任务，转而通过任何必要的手段实现目标时）；奖励黑客（当模型找到一种方法来最大化其分数而不实际做它被告知要做的工作时）；并逃避监管（在这种情况下，OpenAI 似乎没有人意识到整个周末发生了什么）。 所有这些都是最新事件的一部分。

至于接下来会发生什么，有迹象表明我们不会经历新闻报道中山姆·奥尔特曼照片的低迷。 Hugging Face 的联合创始人表示，这应该给行业敲响“警钟”。 或许！ 正如我之前提到的，在大学时，我有一个朋友每隔 10 分钟就按一下闹钟，持续了八个半小时，这感觉很像这个特定行业经历的警钟。 当下一场比赛结束时，我一定会起床，说真的，我保证……

  • 玛丽娜·海德是《卫报》专栏作家
